user_query:
I'm setting up a small, event‑triggered workflow for our information literacy program. Please build a script or small automation that watches a local inbox for new topic list files and, when one appears, collects a few authoritative web resources per topic for offline review.

Please use the provided input/topics.txt as the seed list for a test run, and structure the job so it would work the same way for any future drop-in text file.

Requirements:
1) Event trigger and folder layout
- Create these directories if they don’t exist: watch/inbox, watch/processed, outputs, outputs/raw, outputs/processed.
- Simulate a new drop by copying input/topics.txt into watch/inbox as watch/inbox/topics_drop.txt before your scan begins.
- The automation should then scan watch/inbox for any *.txt files not yet processed and treat each such file as a trigger event. After successful processing, move the file to watch/processed.
- Do not reprocess a file if a matching record exists in outputs/processed/<filename>.json.

2) Parsing the topics file
- Each input file is newline‑separated topics (ignore empty lines and lines starting with #). For the test, process watch/inbox/topics_drop.txt (copied from input/topics.txt).
- For each topic, derive a topic_slug: lowercase ASCII, trim, replace non‑alphanumerics with single hyphens, and strip leading/trailing hyphens (e.g., "Primary vs secondary sources" → "primary-vs-secondary-sources").

3) Web search and download
- For each topic, perform a web search using a public search engine (no API key). Construct a query of the form: "<topic> site:edu OR site:gov OR site:ala.org OR site:ifla.org".
- Collect up to 3 distinct result URLs per topic that match allowed domains (.edu, .gov, ala.org, ifla.org). If fewer than 3 are available, use what you find. Skip results from other domains.
- For each selected URL, download the page. Save the raw HTML and an extracted plain‑text version:
  - outputs/raw/<topic_slug>/<n>_raw.html (raw bytes decoded as UTF‑8 if possible)
  - outputs/raw/<topic_slug>/<n>.txt (plain text extracted by stripping HTML tags)

4) Structured logs and summaries
- Append one CSV row per fetched page to outputs/search_log.csv with the following columns (in this order): file_id, topic, topic_slug, query, url, domain, http_status, title, chars, words, sha256_html, retrieved_at_iso.
  - file_id should be the inbox filename (e.g., topics_drop.txt).
  - title is the page’s <title> text if available; else empty.
  - chars and words are counts of the extracted plain text.
  - sha256_html is the SHA‑256 of the raw HTML bytes (hex).
  - retrieved_at_iso is an ISO‑8601 timestamp of retrieval.
- Write a summary JSON at outputs/index.json containing an array of objects with fields: topic, topic_slug, total_sources, urls (list of strings). Aggregate across all processed topics in the run.
- For each processed inbox file, write a receipt JSON to outputs/processed/<filename>.json with fields: filename, processed_topics (count), total_sources (count), processed_at_iso.
- Log any errors or skips to outputs/errors.log.

5) Constraints
- Only download pages whose domains match: .edu, .gov, ala.org, ifla.org.
- Be tolerant of zero or fewer than 3 results for a given topic; still record what was attempted and skip cleanly on failures (non‑200 status should still generate a CSV row with http_status and empty text counts).
- The Internet steps (searching and downloading) are required parts of this task; do not hardcode URLs.

Deliverables to validate:
- outputs/search_log.csv with at least some rows corresponding to topics in input/topics.txt.
- outputs/index.json summarizing topics and collected URLs.
- outputs/raw/<topic_slug>/ files as described.
- outputs/processed/topics_drop.txt.json and watch/processed/topics_drop.txt.
- outputs/errors.log (can be empty if no errors occurred).

Input path: input/topics.txt (provided). All outputs must be under outputs/ as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
